Title: Anthocyanin Biosynthesis and DNA Methylation Dynamics in Sweet Orange Fruit [Citrus sinensis L. (Osbeck)] under Cold Stress
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: contributes to blood red color, organoleptic quality, and nutritional properties of fruit
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with cold-induced anthocyanin accumulation by promoting promoter demethylation of anthocyanin-related genes in HP flesh areas.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by cold in HP fruit flesh areas, especially at 10 and 20 days of storage.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in HP than NP fruit flesh during cold storage at specific sampling points.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold storage at 4 °C, 90–95% RH, for up to 25 days.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Gene itself is implicated in active DNA demethylation; increased DML1 expression is associated with decreased cytosine methylation at DFR and Ruby promoters in HP areas under cold stress.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling; correlation with promoter methylation analysis by McrBC-qPCR/MSAP and anthocyanin measurement.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DML1 expression was induced by cold in highly pigmented fruit flesh segments. 2. Its induction correlated with progressive demethylation of DFR and Ruby promoters. 3. The study suggests DML1 as the main demethylase contributing to cold-induced anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inference is correlative; DML1 role was suggested but not directly validated by mutant or transgenic manipulation in this paper.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not supported as a positive driver of cold-induced anthocyanin accumulation; expression was repressed in HP areas.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Repressed by cold in HP segments compared with NP from 15 days onward.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower in HP than NP during later cold storage.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DME transcript levels decreased in HP areas under cold. 2. The expression trend was inconsistent with the demethylation pattern linked to anthocyanin induction. 3. The study does not support DME as the main demethylase involved in this response.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because demethylase genes were experimentally tested in Results, but evidence argues against a major positive role in the focal metabolic response.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not supported as a positive driver of cold-induced anthocyanin accumulation; expression was repressed in HP areas.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Repressed by cold in HP segments compared with NP from 15 days onward.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower in HP than NP during later cold storage.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DML3 expression decreased in HP areas during cold storage. 2. Its expression pattern does not match the promoter demethylation associated with anthocyanin induction. 3. The study does not implicate DML3 as the major demethylase in the process.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Experimentally analyzed but not supported as decisive for the focal metabolic phenotype.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not supported as a positive driver of cold-induced anthocyanin accumulation; expression was repressed in HP areas.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Repressed by cold in HP segments compared with NP from 15 days onward.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower in HP than NP during later cold storage.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DML4 transcript abundance was reduced in HP segments during cold storage. 2. The expression profile was not consistent with the cold-induced demethylation linked to anthocyanin accumulation. 3. The study suggests DML4 is unlikely to be the main demethylase responsible.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Experimentally analyzed but not supported as decisive for the focal metabolic phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for anthocyanin biosynthesis and branch flux control
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as an important regulatory point controlling flux into branches leading to distinct anthocyanin profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher DFR expression and lower DFR promoter methylation are associated with higher anthocyanin accumulation in HP flesh under cold stress.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in HP flesh during cold storage; peaked around 15 days at about 3–4-fold above NP.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in HP flesh under cold stress.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold storage at 4 °C; comparison of HP vs NP segments of the same fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter cytosine methylation decreased strongly in HP areas during cold storage, but increased in NP areas; hypomethylation correlated with higher transcription.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling; promoter methylation analysis by McrBC-qPCR/MSAP; anthocyanin quantification correlation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DFR transcript abundance was markedly higher in HP than NP flesh during cold storage. 2. DFR promoter methylation decreased to very low levels in HP areas but increased in NP areas. 3. DFR is implicated as a key flux-controlling structural gene associated with cold-induced anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes DFR for promoter methylation analysis because it is an important regulatory point in the pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-induced PAL expression in HP flesh is associated with increased anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increased from 5 days and peaked at 15 days in HP areas at >4-fold of NP.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in HP flesh under cold stress.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling correlated with anthocyanin measurement.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL expression increased early during cold storage in HP flesh. 2. Peak transcript levels occurred around 15 days and exceeded those in NP tissue. 3. Increased PAL expression paralleled anthocyanin accumulation in pigmented sectors.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter methylation was not measured for PAL in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-induced CHS expression in HP flesh is associated with increased anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Enhanced from the second sampling and peaked at 15 days at roughly 3–4-fold above NP.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in HP flesh under cold stress.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling correlated with anthocyanin measurement.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS transcripts increased in HP flesh during cold storage. 2. Expression peaked around 15 days and exceeded NP levels by several fold. 3. CHS induction paralleled anthocyanin accumulation in pigmented tissue.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter methylation was not measured for CHS in this study.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate anthocyanidin precursors of anthocyanin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-induced ANS expression in HP flesh is associated with increased anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Enhanced from the second sampling and peaked at 15 days at roughly 3–4-fold above NP.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in HP flesh under cold stress.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling correlated with anthocyanin measurement.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ANS transcripts increased in HP flesh during cold exposure. 2. Expression exceeded NP levels and paralleled rising anthocyanin content. 3. ANS is part of the coordinated structural gene induction in pigmented tissue.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter methylation was not measured for ANS in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-induced UFGT expression in HP flesh is associated with increased anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Enhanced from the second sampling and peaked at 15 days at roughly 3–4-fold above NP.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in HP flesh under cold stress.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling correlated with anthocyanin measurement.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UFGT expression increased markedly in HP flesh during cold storage. 2. Transcript accumulation paralleled anthocyanin increase in pigmented sectors. 3. UFGT participates in the cold-induced activation of the late anthocyanin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter methylation was not measured for UFGT in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: red pigmentation, organoleptic and nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator enabling anthocyanin pigmentation in blood orange and strongly associated with cold-induced flesh pigmentation in HP areas.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: anthocyanin structural genes including PAL; CHS; DFR; ANS; UFGT
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Higher Ruby expression in HP areas is associated with enhanced expression of anthocyanin structural genes.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions within the MBW complex required for activation of the anthocyanin biosynthetic pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cold stress; promoter transposon insertion background in blood orange
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increased in HP fruit flesh during cold storage, peaking at 15 days at >3-fold above NP.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in HP flesh under cold stress.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter cytosine methylation strongly decreased in HP areas during cold storage but increased in NP areas; demethylation correlated with increased Ruby transcription.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cold-induced anthocyanin accumulation in blood orange flesh; higher expression in HP areas corresponds to stronger pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling; promoter methylation analysis by McrBC-qPCR/MSAP; anthocyanin quantification correlation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ruby expression was specifically enhanced in highly pigmented flesh areas during cold storage. 2. Ruby promoter methylation progressively decreased in HP areas but increased in NP areas. 3. Ruby activation correlates with induction of structural anthocyanin genes and higher anthocyanin content.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes that blood orange pigmentation is exclusively achieved because of a transposon insertion at the Ruby promoter permitting expression.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not decisive for cold-induced anthocyanin accumulation in fruit flesh in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: No differential regulatory effect detected between HP and NP areas under cold stress.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Previously associated with MBW-related regulation in Citrus, but not supported here as a differential controller of flesh pigmentation under cold.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: No significant expression difference between HP and NP flesh during the whole cold storage period.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in fruit flesh but not differentially responsive between HP and NP under the tested condition.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No evident involvement in cold-induced pigmentation differences between HP and NP flesh segments.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Noemi was expressed in sweet orange flesh. 2. Its transcript level did not differ between HP and NP segments during cold storage. 3. The study concludes Noemi is likely not involved in cold-induced anthocyanin accumulation in fruit flesh.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note Noemi is correlated in Citrus with anthocyanin production in leaves and buds and with fruit acidity, but not with flesh pigmentation under cold stress here.
